---
title: "Project 2 Report: Interactive Data Wrangling Studio"
author: "Jason Zhao, Team Member 1, Team Member 2, Team Member3"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: false
    number_sections: false
---

1. Project Overview

In modern data science, the ability to transition from raw data to actionable insights requires robust preprocessing and exploratory tools. This application bridges that gap using the R Shiny framework, offering a scalable and user-friendly environment for dynamic data manipulation. It is designed to support operations such as data uploading, cleaning, preprocessing, feature engineering, exploratory data analysis, and data export. This application targets all Advanced criteria set forth in the project rubrics, ensuring a professional-grade experience for stakeholders.

2. Core App Functionalities

2.1. Multi-Format Data Integration

The app supports a wide range of data inputs to ensure maximum flexibility for users:

File Formats: Supports CSV, Excel (.xlsx/.xls), JSON, and RDS files.

Built-in Datasets: Provides three diverse datasets (iris, mtcars, and ToothGrowth) for immediate testing and demonstration.

2.2. Interactive Cleaning & Preprocessing

To handle "messy" real-world data, the app implements a reactive cleaning pipeline:

Text Standardization: Automatically normalizes column names and cleans inconsistent text tokens (e.g., converting "Yes/No/True/1" to a standard format).

Missing Value Management: Offers a "Smart Impute" system allowing users to choose between Mean, Median, or Zero for numeric columns, and Mode or Labels for categorical data.

Outlier & Scaling: Includes IQR-based outlier capping/removal and multiple scaling methods (Standard, Min-Max, and Robust) to prepare data for machine learning.

2.3. Advanced Feature Engineering

Users can generate new analytical insights through an interactive "Recipe" system:

Operations: Supports arithmetic (add, subtract, multiply, divide) and mathematical transformations (log, square root, absolute value).

Real-time Feedback: Includes a distribution preview that updates instantly as new features are added, allowing users to see the statistical impact of their transformations.

2.4. Interactive EDA & Visualization

The Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) module utilizes Plotly for high-fidelity, interactive visualizations:

Chart Types: Dynamic generation of Histograms, Box plots, Violin plots, Scatter plots (with trend lines), and Bar charts.

Dynamic Insights: A "Statistical Insights" panel displays key metrics like Skewness, Kurtosis, and Quartiles for the selected variable.

Correlation Matrix: A Pearson correlation heatmap with annotated values for identifying variable relationships.

3. User Interface (UI) and Experience (UX)

The application is built with a focus on responsiveness and professional aesthetics:

Guided Workflow: A dedicated User Guide tab provides a step-by-step "Workflow Badge" system to assist first-time users.

Metric Cards: Real-time counters for rows, columns, and missing values provide an immediate "Before vs. After" comparison of data quality.

Responsive Design: Built using bslib and custom CSS to ensure the layout remains functional across various screen sizes

4. How to Use the App

Users first launch the application, where they can choose to utilize a built-in dataset or upload a custom file. Once the data has finished loading, users can proceed sequentially through steps such as data cleaning, preprocessing, and feature engineering. At each stage, the application automatically updates the current dataset, allowing users to monitor the status of the data via the "Preview" panel. Users can visualize the processed data using the "EDA" tab, while the final dataset can be downloaded from the "Export" tab.

Recommended workflow: 

Load: Upload a file or select a built-in dataset in the Load Data tab.

Clean: Navigate to Cleaning to standardize text and handle missing values.

Process: Use Preprocessing to scale numeric features or encode categorical variables.

Engineer: Create new variables in Feature Engineering using the operation menu.

Explore: Configure plots and filters in the ED  tab to find patterns.

Export: Download your final, processed dataset as a CSV in the Export tab.

5. Deployment and Contributions

Deployment: The application was deployed online using shinyapps.io.

Source Code: Deployed application link: https://5243project.shinyapps.io/project2-data-wrangling/

Team Member Contributions:

Member

Primary Responsibility

Key Deliverables

Janson Zhao

Architect Developer

Design app structure, implemented the Shiny user interface, and organized the multi-tab workflow.

Charlene Shen

Feature & UI Engineer

Extend the app’s UI/UX analytical capabilities by adding advanced visualizations, interpretable feature engineering, and interactive comparison tools, transforming it into a more user-friendly and product-level data application.

Xiao Xiao

Visualization Lead

Testing, Bug Fixing, and Final Logic Audit

Yikai Zhou

Documentation Lead

Template Architecture, Logic Audit, Technical Report

Conclusion

In summary, this project produced a complete Shiny application for interactive data wrangling and exploration. The app integrates data upload, cleaning, preprocessing, feature engineering, visualization, and export in a single workflow. Its deployment on shinyapps.io also makes it easy to demonstrate and evaluate as a web-based data application.

## Conclusion

In summary, this project produced a complete Shiny application for interactive data wrangling and exploration. The app integrates data upload, cleaning, preprocessing, feature engineering, visualization, and export in a single workflow. Its deployment on shinyapps.io also makes it easy to demonstrate and evaluate as a web-based data application.
